name: combination-discovery
purpose: Generate ranked candidate formulations that satisfy requirements and constraints, driven by mechanism compatibility — every candidate an unvalidated hypothesis.

transformation.statement: Requirements + Constraints (+ Knowledge Base) -> ranked candidate formulations, all unvalidated hypotheses

consumes.name: requirements
consumes.description: Target goals: required properties (e.g. fire class A1), performance targets, and weighted objectives (Value Field).

targets.description: Desired properties/goals, e.g. { property:'fire_class', target:'A1' } or { property:'voc', target:'low' }.

consumes.name: constraints
consumes.description: Hard limits: cost ceiling, banned/required materials, available raw materials, regulatory and geo limits.

allowedMaterials.description: If set, candidates draw only from these (e.g. Fresco inventory).

produces.name: candidates
produces.description: A ranked set of candidate formulations. EVERY candidate is a hypothesis carrying provenance, assumptions, confidence type and the validation it still needs. The engine may never emit a candidate lacking these (enforced by required fields below).

truncated.description: True if the space was capped — the set is not exhaustive.

domain.description: Whether this material's data came from external IKL or internal Fresco knowledge.

predicted.description: Predicted properties WITH uncertainty — never point claims.

mechanismRationale.description: Why the mechanisms suggest this could work (the reasoning trace).

ranking.description: Score breakdown: compatibility, mechanism, risk, confidence, expected performance, cost, novelty.

confidenceType.description: PROXY — this is a mechanism-plausibility prior, not a validated probability. See finding F4.

epistemicStatus.description: Hard-locked: a generated candidate is NEVER a fact.

assumptions.description: Explicit assumptions the candidate rests on (e.g. 'assumes silane–carbonate compatibility from TDS X, untested at this dosage').

validationRequirements.description: What experiment(s) would confirm this — the missing validation. Feeds the Autonomous Experiment Planner (Engine 23).

provenance.description: The KB facts (mechanisms, compatibility rows, raw-material/perf records with source ids) the candidate was built from.

reasoning.evidenceModel: mechanism-constrained plausibility: candidates are pruned/scored by compatibility + mechanism support, NOT by experiment. No candidate is tested.
reasoning.notes: Confidence here is an UNVALIDATED plausibility prior — fundamentally weaker than KEE's independent_evidence or a lab result. 'model_probability' is a proxy; a generative confidenceType is a v0.3 need (finding F4). Uncertainty is exposed per predicted property, never hidden.

confidence.notes: Plausibility, not probability of success. Low by construction until validated.

cost.notes: Mechanism-pruned search over a bounded space (maxCandidates), NOT brute-force enumeration. Cost scales with the pruned frontier, not the full combinatorial space.

failureModes.result: rejected with an explanation; no candidates

failureModes.condition: space too large to explore fully
failureModes.result: returns a capped, mechanism-pruned set with generationBounds.truncated=true + warning

failureModes.condition: KB lacks mechanism/compatibility data for the space
failureModes.result: fewer candidates, elevated uncertainty, emits knowledge-gap flags (feeds Engine 22)

provenance.notes: Each candidate cites the KB facts it was assembled from AND its assumptions and required validation. A candidate without provenance+assumptions+validationRequirements is structurally impossible (required in the produces schema). This is how a generative engine stays honest.
